target.title: Expose model picker agent runtimes
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds additive `agentRuntime` metadata to `models.list` model choices when the model/provider has an explicit configured agent runtime.
- Shows non-default runtime labels in the WebUI model picker (for example `GPT-5.5 · OpenAI Codex`) without changing the canonical model value (`openai/gpt-5.5`).
- Adds focused gateway helper and UI label regressions for the OpenAI/Codex route split.

Refs #90420 (real 6.1 migration/runtime-observability report) and #84032 (diamond-lobster issue: model picker/provider/runtime ambiguity). Related to #90036 and #90047 because the live Windows 6.1 route incident made the same ambiguity operationally visible: the healthy route was `openai/gpt-5.5` + Codex runtime, while stale durable state still surfaced as `openai-codex/gpt-5.5`.

## Why

After the 2026.6.1 Codex migration, a healthy route can be:

```text
model ref: openai/gpt-5.5
runtime: codex
```

The picker previously only exposed/displayed provider/model metadata, so operators could not tell whether an `openai/*` model was configured to run through Codex or through the default OpenClaw route. This PR keeps the user-facing model ref stable while exposing the configured runtime separately.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/chat-model-ref.test.ts --testNamePattern "surfaces configured agent runtime metadata"`
- `node --import tsx` behavior script for `addConfiguredAgentRuntimeMetadata` → `MODELS_LIST_RUNTIME_METADATA_OK`
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm tsgo:test:ui`

Note: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/models-list-result.test.ts --testNamePattern "runtime metadata"` and `pnpm tsgo:test:src` were attempted locally but remained silent/slow in this checkout and were stopped; the focused helper behavior was verified directly and CI should run the normal suite.


## Review fix proof

Addressed ClawSweeper's implicit-runtime feedback in `a971a356`.

Focused proof from the patched branch:

```text
implicitCodex.agentRuntime = { id: "codex", source: "implicit", label: "OpenAI Codex" }
implicitDefault.agentRuntime = omitted
explicitOpenClaw.agentRuntime = { id: "openclaw", source: "model", label: "OpenClaw Default" }
labels = ["GPT-5.5 · OpenAI Codex", "GPT-5.5 · OpenClaw Default"]
RUNTIME_METADATA_REVIEW_FIX_OK

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/chat-model-ref.test.ts --testNamePattern "agent runtime|OpenClaw runtime"
✓ ui/src/ui/chat-model-ref.test.ts (25 tests | 23 skipped)
Tests 2 passed | 23 skipped

git diff --check
```

The picker now distinguishes both default implicit OpenAI → Codex routing and explicit OpenClaw runtime overrides while still omitting compact implicit default/OpenClaw/auto metadata for unrelated providers.

## Real behavior proof (structured for gate)

- Behavior or issue addressed: expose material agent runtime metadata for `openai/gpt-*` models routed through Codex so operators can distinguish `openai/gpt-5.5` + Codex from a plain OpenClaw/default runtime in the model picker.
- Real environment tested: real Windows/ROG OpenClaw `2026.6.1` migration context where the healthy route is `openai/gpt-5.5` plus Codex runtime, plus this PR branch on macOS source checkout.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: ran the patched WebUI picker regression with `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/chat-model-ref.test.ts --testNamePattern="agent runtime|OpenClaw runtime|surfaces configured agent runtime metadata"` and a focused helper probe for `addConfiguredAgentRuntimeMetadata`.
- Evidence after fix: copied live terminal output:

```text
Focused helper output from patched branch:
implicitCodex.agentRuntime = { id: "codex", source: "implicit", label: "OpenAI Codex" }
implicitDefault.agentRuntime = omitted
explicitOpenClaw.agentRuntime = { id: "openclaw", source: "model", label: "OpenClaw Default" }
labels = ["GPT-5.5 · OpenAI Codex", "GPT-5.5 · OpenClaw Default"]
RUNTIME_METADATA_REVIEW_FIX_OK

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/chat-model-ref.test.ts --testNamePattern="agent runtime|OpenClaw runtime|surfaces configured agent runtime metadata"
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.ui.config.ts
✓ ui ui/src/ui/chat-model-ref.test.ts (25 tests | 23 skipped) 22ms
Tests 2 passed | 23 skipped (25)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 3.78s
```

- Observed result after fix: model choices keep the canonical value `openai/gpt-5.5` while exposing/displaying `OpenAI Codex` or explicit `OpenClaw Default` runtime metadata where it materially changes operator understanding.
- What was not tested: production hot-deploy of the WebUI picker path was not performed; the dedicated WebUI visual proof below uses this PR branch in a mock gateway to isolate the user-visible picker rendering. Commentary/progress streams, provider/TaaS/cache plugin metadata, and session migration behavior are intentionally out of scope.


## Real behavior proof (WebUI model picker visual proof)

- Behavior or issue addressed: the user-visible model picker should render the runtime-decorated label `GPT-5.5 · OpenAI Codex` for a canonical `openai/gpt-5.5` model choice carrying `agentRuntime.label = "OpenAI Codex"`, without changing the selected model value away from `openai/gpt-5.5`.
- Real environment tested: this PR branch head `5e26af0e01275904a90e7982d817168f0d422994` in a local source checkout at `/tmp/openclaw-pr-90328-update` (`package.json` version `2026.6.2`), served through the Control UI mock dev server with the PR branch WebUI code. This visual proof did **not** use the installed/running Mac Gateway (`OpenClaw 2026.5.3`) and did **not** use the Windows/Youyou Gateway; it isolates the PR branch WebUI rendering path. The mock gateway model catalog supplied `id: "openai/gpt-5.5"`, `provider: "openai"`, and `agentRuntime: { id: "codex", label: "OpenAI Codex", source: "configured" }`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
cd /tmp/openclaw-pr-90328-update
git rev-parse HEAD  # 5e26af0e01275904a90e7982d817168f0d422994
node -p "require('./package.json').version"  # 2026.6.2
pnpm dev:ui:mock -- --host 127.0.0.1 --port 5193
# Open http://127.0.0.1:5193/chat, open the Chat model picker, and capture the picker state.
CI=true pnpm protocol:check
```

- Evidence after fix: visual artifact and generated-protocol verification:
  - Screenshot artifact: [https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ooiuuii/openclaw/refs/heads/proof/90328-model-picker-20260606/proof/90328/model-picker-openai-codex.png](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ooiuuii/openclaw/refs/heads/proof/90328-model-picker-20260606/proof/90328/model-picker-openai-codex.png)
  - Artifact README: [https://github.com/ooiuuii/openclaw/tree/proof/90328-model-picker-20260606/proof/90328](https://github.com/ooiuuii/openclaw/tree/proof/90328-model-picker-20260606/proof/90328)
  - The screenshot shows the open Chat model picker with the option `GPT-5.5 · OpenAI Codex` visible between `Default model` and `claude-sonnet-4-6`.
  - `CI=true pnpm protocol:check` regenerated protocol/Swift artifacts and exited cleanly with no final-head diff.
- Observed result after fix: the WebUI picker renders `GPT-5.5 · OpenAI Codex` for the runtime-decorated canonical OpenAI model choice while preserving the canonical `openai/gpt-5.5` value supplied by `models.list`.
- What was not tested: this visual proof uses the Control UI mock gateway rather than the installed Mac 2026.5.3 Gateway, the Windows/Youyou Gateway, or a production Gateway session. That is intentional so the screenshot isolates this PR branch's picker rendering behavior; the separate Gateway proof below covers the actual `models.list` response shape from the PR branch.


## Real behavior proof (Windows 6.1 route incident + patched models.list metadata)

- Behavior or issue addressed: a real upgraded Windows/Telegram OpenClaw setup used the healthy canonical model ref `openai/gpt-5.5` with Codex runtime routing, but the UI/protocol had no runtime metadata surface, making it hard to distinguish the healthy `openai/gpt-5.5 + OpenAI Codex` route from stale or default-runtime cases.
- Real environment tested: redacted Windows/ROG OpenClaw `2026.6.1 (2e08f0f)` Telegram/dashboard setup that previously hit `Unknown model: openai-codex/gpt-5.5`, plus this PR branch on a local macOS source checkout using the same canonical route shape.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: repaired the real Windows setup to canonical `openai/gpt-5.5` plus `agentRuntime.id = "codex"` without clearing OAuth, verified the real route with health/models/smoke checks, then ran this PR branch's `addConfiguredAgentRuntimeMetadata(...)` over the same redacted route shape to inspect the `models.list` choice metadata.
- Evidence after fix: copied live/redacted output from the real incident and patched branch:

```text
Real Windows 6.1 route after repair, redacted:
- previous live symptom: dashboard/Telegram turns failed with `Unknown model: openai-codex/gpt-5.5`
- repaired config/session route: modelRef = openai/gpt-5.5, agentRuntime.id = codex
- Gateway /health: live
- openclaw models list: openai/gpt-5.5 shown as default/configured
- CLI smoke: SMOKE_OK_601_MODEL_ROUTE
- Telegram direct-session smoke: TELEGRAM_SESSION_SMOKE_OK

Patched PR branch probe for the same route shape:
{
  "realRouteShape": {
    "modelRef": "openai/gpt-5.5",
    "agentRuntime": { "id": "codex" }
  },
  "patchedModelsListChoice": {
    "id": "gpt-5.5",
    "name": "GPT-5.5",
    "provider": "openai",
    "agentRuntime": {
      "id": "codex",
      "source": "model",
      "label": "OpenAI Codex"
    }
  }
}

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/chat-model-ref.test.ts --testNamePattern="agent runtime|OpenClaw runtime|surfaces configured agent runtime metadata"
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.ui.config.ts
✓ ui ui/src/ui/chat-model-ref.test.ts (25 tests | 23 skipped) 22ms
Tests 2 passed | 23 skipped (25)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 3.78s
```

- Observed result after fix: for the real Windows route shape, the patched `models.list` choice carries `agentRuntime.id = "codex"` and label `OpenAI Codex` while preserving the canonical model choice (`provider: "openai"`, `id: "gpt-5.5"`). The picker regression confirms the UI can render that as `GPT-5.5 · OpenAI Codex` instead of hiding the runtime distinction.
- What was not tested: this PR branch was not hot-deployed to the production Windows gateway; the live Windows proof is the real incident/repair route, and the after-patch proof is the PR branch metadata/UI path exercised against that same redacted route shape. Commentary/progress streams and session-store migration/write-boundary behavior remain out of scope for this PR.

## Real behavior proof (PR branch Gateway `models.list` output)

- Behavior or issue addressed: #90420 / #84032 need the model picker and Gateway model list to show when canonical `openai/gpt-5.5` is routed through the Codex runtime, so operators can distinguish a healthy 6.1 route from stale `openai-codex/*` state.
- Real environment tested: this PR branch (`agent/xiaozhua/model-runtime-observability`, OpenClaw `2026.6.2 (ac572e7)`) running an isolated local Gateway with a temporary state dir and `OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1`; the config used canonical `openai/gpt-5.5` with `agents.defaults.models["openai/gpt-5.5"].agentRuntime.id = "codex"`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-pr90328-gw.INQWL3 OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-pr90328-gw.INQWL3/openclaw.json OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1 node scripts/run-node.mjs gateway run --port 19828 --bind loopback --auth none --allow-unconfigured --compact

OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-pr90328-gw.INQWL3 OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-pr90328-gw.INQWL3/openclaw.json node scripts/run-node.mjs gateway health --url ws://127.0.0.1:19828 --token dummy --json --timeout 10000

OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-pr90328-gw.INQWL3 OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-pr90328-gw.INQWL3/openclaw.json node scripts/run-node.mjs gateway call models.list --url ws://127.0.0.1:19828 --token dummy --json --timeout 30000
```

- Evidence after fix:

Gateway health loaded the PR-branch Gateway and the relevant plugins:

```json
{
  "ok": true,
  "plugins": {
    "loaded": ["codex", "memory-core", "openai"],
    "errors": []
  },
  "defaultAgentId": "main"
}
```

Gateway log from the same run:

```text
[gateway] agent model: openai/gpt-5.5 (thinking=medium, fast=off)
[gateway] http server listening (1 plugin: codex; 14.7s)
[ws] ⇄ res ✓ models.list 1137ms
```

Actual PR-branch `models.list` response:

```json
{
  "models": [
    {
      "provider": "openai",
      "id": "gpt-5.5",
      "name": "gpt-5.5",
      "agentRuntime": {
        "id": "codex",
        "source": "model",
        "label": "OpenAI Codex"
      }
    }
  ]
}
```

- Observed result after fix: the public Gateway model-list surface preserves the canonical selected value (`provider: "openai"`, `id: "gpt-5.5"`) while exposing the configured runtime separately as `agentRuntime.id = "codex"` / `label = "OpenAI Codex"`. This is the exact after-fix surface needed for the model picker to show `GPT-5.5 · OpenAI Codex` without reverting to legacy `openai-codex/*` model refs.
- What was not tested: I did not use real OAuth credentials or send a model inference request in this isolated Gateway run. Channels were intentionally skipped and the temporary Gateway was stopped after collecting the `models.list` output; the separate WebUI proof above captures the browser picker rendering.

target.title: fix(feishu): retry on send rate-limit errors (230020/230006)
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds retry logic (up to 2 retries, 500ms/1000ms linear backoff) to `requestFeishuApi` for Feishu send rate-limit error code `230020`.
- Both `im.message.create` (direct send) and `im.message.reply` (reply send) paths now go through retry.
- Exports `getFeishuSendRateLimitCode()` helper to detect retryable codes from AxiosError response shape.
- Adds 22 unit/integration tests covering concurrent send, retry exhaustion, backoff timing, and error message surfacing.

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm test extensions/feishu/src/send.retry.test.ts` — 13 tests pass
- [x] `pnpm test extensions/feishu/src/send.concurrent.test.ts` — 9 tests pass
- [x] Real 20-concurrent direct send stress test: 20/20 success after retry
- [x] Real 15-concurrent reply stress test: 15/15 success after retry

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed**: Feishu plugin now retries transient `230020` rate-limit errors on both direct send (`im.message.create`) and reply (`im.message.reply`) paths, instead of failing immediately and losing messages.

**Real environment tested**: Live Feishu API (`open.feishu.cn`), app `cli_aa947bd28afa5bd9`, macOS local OpenClaw dev build with `npx tsx` runner.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```
# Direct send stress test — 20 concurrent messages to same user
npx tsx scripts/feishu-stress-real.mts

# Reply stress test — 15 concurrent replies to a single seed message
npx tsx scripts/feishu-stress-reply.mts --count=15
```

**Evidence after fix**:

Direct send (`im.message.create`) — 20 concurrent:
```
[config] appId=cli_aa947bd28afa5bd9  target=ou_33af95b6f9c0e5fbcaccef732f03d720  count=20

Firing 20 concurrent sends through requestFeishuApi (with retry) …

  (×10 rate-limit 230020 hits — all retried automatically)

  [10] ✓ 1609ms  msg_id=om_x100b6ec44878f0a0b3c1c92d40adb2d
  [20] ✓ 1613ms  msg_id=om_x100b6ec448782ca0b1c56b3f9cac777
  [11] ✓ 1627ms  msg_id=om_x100b6ec4487850a0b1934d47f23f5ee
  [17] ✓ 2708ms  msg_id=om_x100b6ec4480bc478b1fe39b7f652100

─────────────────── summary ───────────────────
  total     : 20
  succeeded : 20
  failed    : 0
  wall time : 2709 ms
───────────────────────────────────────────────
```

Reply send (`im.message.reply`) — 15 concurrent:
```
[config] appId=cli_aa947bd28afa5bd9  target=ou_33af95b6f9c0e5fbcaccef732f03d720  count=15

[step 1] ✓ seed message_id = om_x100b6ecbc38750b8b29ba53f44a8667
[step 2] Firing 15 concurrent replies through requestFeishuApi (with retry) …

  (×5 rate-limit 230020 hits — all retried and succeeded)

  [01] ✓ 850ms   msg_id=om_x100b6ecbc395fca0b01e118c0d0feea
  [04] ✓ 1112ms  msg_id=om_x100b6ecbc39128acb129b0880a4324d
  [15] ✓ 1192ms  msg_id=om_x100b6ecbc390f0a8b1d802607f077e1
  [06] ✓ 1603ms  msg_id=om_x100b6ecbc3a954b0b4b3d0ce617fa06

─────────────────── reply summary ───────────────────
  total     : 15
  succeeded : 15
  failed    : 0
  wall time : 1604 ms
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
```

**Observed result after fix**:
- Direct send: 10/20 initially rate-limited → retried → **20/20 success**
- Reply send: 5/15 initially rate-limited → retried → **15/15 success**
- Before fix: rate-limited messages were lost permanently with no retry.

**What was not tested**: 20-concurrent reply to the same message exhausts the bounded retry for 4/20 (reply endpoint has stricter per-message rate limit). The 2-retry/linear-backoff design handles normal production concurrency (≤15) without message loss.

Closes #70879

target.title: fix(memory-core): exclude archived transcripts from Dreaming session corpus
target.body:
## Summary

Archived session transcripts (`.jsonl.reset.*` and `.jsonl.deleted.*`) are
currently ingested into the Dreaming corpus, polluting analysis with
reset/deleted cron-spawned subagent noise. On a cron-heavy deployment,
this causes 67-91% of the daily corpus to be dominated by automation
noise, suppressing pattern discovery. This is particularly visible on
workspaces where cron jobs frequently spawn subagents whose transcripts get
reset/deleted but remain in the session directory as usage-counted archives.

## Changes

- Add `isSessionArchiveTranscriptPath` helper that detects `.jsonl.reset.*`
  and `.jsonl.deleted.*` archive transcript files
- Add archive-exclusion filter in `collectSessionIngestionBatches` so
  Dreaming corpus assembly skips archived transcripts
- Preserve `memory_search` archive indexing path unchanged (only Dreaming
  ingestion is affected)
- Align archive transcript regex with canonical `ARCHIVE_TIMESTAMP_RE`:
  the milliseconds portion is now optional `(?:\.\d{3})?Z` instead of
  mandatory `\.\d{3}Z`, matching the session archive artifact contract
  in `src/config/sessions/artifacts.ts`

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed**: Before the fix, `listSessionFilesForAgent` returns
archive transcript files that are ingested into the Dreaming corpus. After the
fix, these are excluded while preserving checkpoint exclusion and
`memory_search` archive indexing.

**Real environment tested**: Running production OpenClaw gateway on Linux
(`/usr/lib/node_modules/openclaw`, gateway port 18789), tested against the
live `/root/.openclaw/agents/` session store. Patch commit `f6f0c61b72`.

**Evidence after fix — real session file classification**:

```
=== Real Session File Classification ===

Total session files scanned: 759
  - Archive files (would be EXCLUDED): 63
  - Normal/checkpoint files (would be INCLUDED): 696

Archive files detected (these enter Dreaming corpus in current code):

  ❌ EXCLUDE: ...agents/hr-agent/sessions/30d9dd9e.jsonl.reset.2026-05-31T02-45-36.108Z
  ❌ EXCLUDE: ...agents/hr-agent/sessions/37325983.jsonl.reset.2026-06-03T08-50-31.129Z
  ❌ EXCLUDE: ...agents/hr-agent/sessions/bcb8108c.jsonl.reset.2026-06-04T01-45-09.140Z
  ... and 60 more across hr-agent, main, market, software-development-engineer

Archive pollution in corpus: 63/759 (8.3%)
After fix: these files are excluded from Dreaming corpus assembly.
```

**Regex edge case verification (includes P3 fix for optional milliseconds)**:

```
  ✅ "session.jsonl.reset.2026-05-14T08-25-07.342Z" → true (expected: true)
  ✅ "session.jsonl.deleted.2026-05-31T23-59-59.999Z" → true (expected: true)
  ✅ "session.jsonl.reset.2026-06-01T12-00-00Z" → true (expected: true)  ← was BROKEN before fix
  ✅ "session.jsonl.deleted.2026-06-01T00-00-00Z" → true (expected: true) ← was BROKEN before fix
  ✅ "session.jsonl" → false (expected: false)
  ✅ "session-checkpoint.jsonl" → false (expected: false)
  ✅ "session.jsonl.bak.2026-06-01T12-00-00.000Z" → false (expected: false)
  ✅ UUID prefix + archive suffix → true (expected: true)
  ✅ "session.jsonl.reset.2026-01-01T00-00-00Z" → true (expected: true)   ← edge: min timestamp, no ms
All cases: PASSED ✅
```

The P3 regex fix addresses the ClawSweeper finding: the local regex now matches
the canonical contract where milliseconds are optional. Cases without `.000Z`
(valid usage-counted archive filenames) are now correctly detected. Direct
import of `isSessionArchiveArtifactName` is not feasible because the plugin SDK
boundary (`openclaw/plugin-sdk/memory-core-host-engine-qmd`) does not re-export
it; the aligned regex achieves the same contract.

**Observed result**: The patch correctly identifies all 63 archive transcript
files on a production workspace with 4 agents (hr-agent, main, market,
software-development-engineer). Normal sessions, checkpoint transcripts, and
`.bak` files are unaffected. The `memory_search` indexing path (which
deliberately includes archive transcripts for post-reset/post-delete search)
is unchanged — only `collectSessionIngestionBatches` (Dreaming corpus
assembly) applies the filter.

## Test Plan

1. `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-phases.test.ts` — 45 tests, all passing
2. `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/session-files.test.ts` — 12 tests, all passing
3. `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-sync-yield.test.ts` — 1 test, passing
4. `pnpm exec oxlint extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-phases.ts` — 0 warnings, 0 errors

## Risk

- Low risk: the change is a purely additive filter in the Dreaming ingestion
  loop. Archive files are still indexed for `memory_search` through the
  unchanged `listSessionFilesForAgent` → `buildSessionEntry` path used by
  `manager-sync-ops.ts` and `qmd-manager.ts`.
- No change to the `memory_search` contract for reset/deleted archive
  indexing.

## Related Issue

Refs #90313 (archive transcript Dreaming corpus pollution).
Note: the broader cron-descended subagent classification issue mentioned in
the linked report is outside this patch's scope and remains tracked separately.

target.title: fix(agents): avoid inviting provider swaps in model alias guidance
target.body:
## Summary
- stop telling the model to prefer aliases when a full provider/model override is already present
- clarify that aliases are only shortcuts for unqualified model requests
- update the prompt test to lock in the exact-ref guidance

## Real behavior proof
Behavior or issue addressed: with model aliases configured, the generated system prompt was telling the model to prefer an alias even when an exact `provider/model` override was already specified.

Real environment tested: a real OpenClaw checkout with the alias config below, comparing the generated system prompt on current `main` versus this branch.

Config used:
```yaml
agents:
  defaults:
    models:
      github-copilot/gpt-4.1:
        alias: copilot-4.1
      openai/gpt-4.1:
        alias: openai-4.1
```

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
1. generated the system prompt with the config above on current `main`
2. generated it again on this branch
3. compared the model-alias guidance line and alias entries

Evidence after fix: copied live output from the generated prompt comparison.
Current `main` says: "Prefer aliases when specifying model overrides; full provider/model is also accepted." It then lists `copilot-4.1: github-copilot/gpt-4.1` and `openai-4.1: openai/gpt-4.1`.
This branch says: "Use exact provider/model strings verbatim when one is specified. Aliases are shortcuts for unqualified model requests." It then lists the same alias mappings.

Observed result after fix: the model-facing prompt no longer tells the model to rewrite an explicit `provider/model` override into an alias.

What was not tested: I did not run a fully deterministic live provider swap scenario because the downstream behavior depends on model choices after reading the prompt; the proof here is the direct before/after prompt output that drives that behavior.

## Testing
- `pnpm vitest src/agents/system-prompt.test.ts`

Closes #61949

target.title: feat(subagents): forward toolsAllow from sessions_spawn
target.body:
## Summary
- Add optional `toolsAllow` to `sessions_spawn` for native embedded subagent runs.
- Validate it as a string array, preserve explicit `[]`, and reject unsupported ACP or CLI-backed native paths instead of silently ignoring the policy.
- Forward and persist the allowlist through Gateway/session/embedded-attempt surfaces so later native child turns inherit the stored tool policy.
- Update protocol/docs/tests/prompt snapshots for the additive field. No `CHANGELOG.md` change is kept.

## Current upstream status
Latest `origin/main` already has broad May subagent/runtime work and existing embedded-runner `toolsAllow` support, but `sessions_spawn` still does not expose, forward, or persist a per-spawn `toolsAllow` entry point. This PR is narrowed to that missing public/control-plane plumbing.

## Change Type (select all)
- [x] Feature

## Scope (select all touched areas)
- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] Documentation

## Linked Issue/PR
- Related #78381
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

Behavior addressed: `sessions_spawn` callers can constrain native embedded subagent tools with `toolsAllow`; the same policy is persisted on child sessions and inherited by later embedded child turns when no explicit override is passed.

Real environment tested: macOS 26.2, Node v25.9.0, pnpm 11.2.2, local OpenClaw source checkout, current PR head `33898746803396a1d3935b28c8d99b6784ae7fc7`, temporary `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, temporary `openclaw.json`, `node scripts/run-node.mjs agent --local`, and repo mock OpenAI `/v1/responses` server with request logging.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Seeded a temp `sessions.json` child session at `agent:main:subagent:tools-policy-proof-470c152139` with `runtimeToolsAllow: ["read", "exec"]`, started `scripts/e2e/mock-openai-server.mjs` with `MOCK_REQUEST_LOG`, then ran `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-78441-proof-current.itWvhi/state OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-78441-proof-current.itWvhi/state/openclaw.json OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-openclaw-78441-proof node scripts/run-node.mjs agent --local --session-key agent:main:subagent:tools-policy-proof-470c152139 --message "Reply with exactly OPENCLAW_E2E_OK_78441" --json`, followed by a Node assertion over the mock request log.

Evidence after fix: Copied live output from the request-log assertion:

```json
{
  "head": "33898746803396a1d3935b28c8d99b6784ae7fc7",
  "sessionKey": "agent:main:subagent:tools-policy-proof-338987468",
  "responsesCalls": 1,
  "inheritedToolNames": [
    "exec",
    "read"
  ],
  "markerObserved": true
}
```



Current-head verification commands after this patch:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.lifecycle.test.ts src/agents/subagent-spawn.model-session.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts --reporter=dot
2 Vitest shards passed: 215 tests passed
```

Observed result after fix: The later CLI turn succeeded, reached the mock `/v1/responses` endpoint exactly once, returned `OPENCLAW_E2E_OK_78441`, and the provider request exposed only the inherited `exec` and `read` tools from the persisted child-session `runtimeToolsAllow` field.

What was not tested: This proof used a mock OpenAI server instead of live OpenAI credentials and did not start a long-running Gateway process. Focused tests cover the `sessions_spawn` writer/forwarding path; this proof verifies the real later-turn CLI/provider request behavior.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)
- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [x] Prompt snapshot check
- Target test or file: `src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts`, `src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.lifecycle.test.ts`, `src/agents/subagent-spawn.model-session.test.ts`, `src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts`, `src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts`, `src/commands/agent.test.ts`, `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts`, `test/fixtures/agents/prompt-snapshots/codex-runtime-happy-path/*`
- Scenario the tests lock in: `toolsAllow` is exposed, normalized, validates string arrays, preserves explicit `[]`, rejects unsupported runtimes, forwards through Gateway/session state, reaches embedded runs started by the command runner, and appears in model-facing dynamic tool snapshots.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes
`runtime="subagent"` callers may pass `toolsAllow` to narrow a spawned native embedded subagent's tool set. Defaults are unchanged when omitted. `toolsAllow: []` intentionally runs the child without tools. Unsupported ACP and CLI-backed native paths reject the option clearly.

## Security Impact (required)
- New permissions/capabilities? `No`
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No`
- New/changed network calls? `No`
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `Yes`
- Data access scope changed? `No`
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: this only narrows the child tool set for native embedded subagent runs. Unsupported paths reject the option so callers do not get a false sense that an allowlist was enforced.

## Repro + Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.lifecycle.test.ts src/agents/subagent-spawn.model-session.test.ts src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts src/commands/agent.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts --reporter=dot` passed: 5 files, 159 tests, 3 Vitest shards.
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo` passed.
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check ...changed files...` passed.
- `pnpm prompt:snapshots:check` passed: `Prompt snapshots are current (7 files)`.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts --reporter=dot src/agents/code-mode.test.ts` passed locally after CI showed a timeout/interrupt flake in that unrelated file: 1 file, 48 tests.
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD && git diff --check` passed.
- `pnpm build` passed before the snapshot commit; `pnpm openclaw agent --help` rebuilt dist successfully after the snapshot commit because the CLI detected a new git head.
- Current-head CLI/mock OpenAI proof above passed.

## Human Verification (required)
- Verified scenarios: schema exposure, native forwarding path, Gateway forwarding path, session persistence, embedded command runner propagation, persisted child-session allowlist inheritance, strict validation tests, ACP rejection tests, CLI-backed native fail-closed behavior for non-empty and empty allowlists, embedded empty-allowlist behavior, prompt snapshot update, formatter/typecheck/build/whitespace checks.
- Edge cases checked: omitted `toolsAllow`, explicit empty `[]`, blank strings trimmed/filtered, non-string entries rejected, non-array input rejected, ACP runtime rejected, CLI-backed native runtime rejected.
- What you did **not** verify: a live provider-backed spawned child run with real OpenAI credentials.

## Compatibility / Migration
- Backward compatible? `Yes`
- Config/env changes? `No`
- Migration needed? `No`

## Risks and Mitigations
- Risk: callers may expect `toolsAllow` to affect ACP runtime spawns.
  - Mitigation: the tool rejects `toolsAllow` for `runtime="acp"` with a clear input error.
- Risk: callers may expect `toolsAllow` to affect CLI-backed native runs.
  - Mitigation: CLI-backed native runs reject `toolsAllow` before dispatch because they cannot enforce the embedded-runner allowlist.
- Risk: public API naming/semantics need maintainer judgment.
  - Mitigation: the implementation is additive and reuses the existing embedded-runner allowlist semantics rather than adding a second filtering mechanism.

target.title: fix(memory-core): write meta after sync when identity is missing with existing chunks
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Gateway memory auto-sync creates a dead loop: it indexes files (creating chunks) but never writes the `memory_index_meta_v1` meta table entry when `indexIdentity.status !== "valid"` and `!needsFullReindex`. All `memory_search` calls return "index metadata is missing" with 0 results.
- Why it matters: Users with fresh or corrupted index state cannot use memory search even though chunks exist in the database.
- What changed: When the early-return path in `runSync()` detects identity is missing but existing chunks prevent a full reindex, the fix forces a verified full reindex via `runSafeReindex`/`runUnsafeReindex` instead of writing unverified meta. The reindex path prunes stale chunks from deleted paths, old providers, or old scopes before writing meta and making the index searchable.

## Design note on stale-row safety

The recovery path now triggers a full reindex rather than stamping fresh metadata onto unverified chunks. The reindex path (`runSafeReindex`/`runUnsafeReindex`) scans current sources, indexes only matching files, prunes stale rows from deleted paths, and writes meta only after verification. This eliminates the risk of making stale, out-of-scope, or cross-provider chunks searchable.

The recovery is intentionally a full reindex rather than a lightweight meta write: the index is already in a broken state (missing meta with orphaned chunks), and a verified rebuild is the safest way to return to a consistent state.

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix

## Scope

- [x] Memory / storage

## Linked Issues

- Closes #90338
- Related: #90361

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** Memory index meta never written when auto-sync finds identity missing with existing chunks (#90338). Every sync cycle indexes files, creates chunks, returns early without writing meta — dead loop. The fix triggers a verified full reindex in this recovery path at `manager-sync-ops.ts:1857-1868`, which prunes stale rows and writes meta only after verification.

**Real environment tested:** Node v22.22.0, Linux x86_64, OpenClaw 2026.6.1.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

Step 1 — manager-level tests (dead-loop recovery + stale-row safety):

```
OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=4 pnpm test -- extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager.fts-only-reindex.test.ts
```

Step 2 — full memory test suite:

```
OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=4 pnpm test -- extensions/memory-core/src/memory/index.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager.fts-only-reindex.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-sync-yield.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-targeted-sync.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager.session-reindex.test.ts
```

**Evidence after fix (terminal capture):**

Dead-loop condition reproduced and fixed — inline SQLite proof (console output):

```
$ node --import tsx/esm -e "
const {openMemoryDatabaseAtPath}=await import('./extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-db.js');
const db=openMemoryDatabaseAtPath('/tmp/deadloop3.db',true);
db.exec('CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS meta(key TEXT PRIMARY KEY, value TEXT); CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS chunks(id TEXT PRIMARY KEY, path TEXT NOT NULL, source TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT ''memory'', text TEXT NOT NULL);');
db.prepare('INSERT INTO chunks VALUES(?,?,?,?)').run('c1','/workspace/notes.md','memory','architecture notes');
// Simulate hasIndexedChunks() — same query as manager.ts:701
const hc=db.prepare('SELECT 1 as found FROM chunks LIMIT 1').get();
const m=db.prepare('SELECT value FROM meta WHERE key=?').get('memory_index_meta_v1');
console.log('hasIndexedChunks:',hc?'true':'false','| meta:',m?'PRESENT':'MISSING (dead loop!)');
db.close();
"
hasIndexedChunks: true | meta: MISSING (dead loop!)
```

Manager-level tests — dead-loop recovery + stale-row safety (9 tests pass, 2 new):

```
$ OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=4 pnpm test -- extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager.fts-only-reindex.test.ts

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  9 passed (9)
   Start at  16:54:56
   Duration  5.81s
exit code: 0
```

Full memory test suite (console output — 5 files, 48 tests):

```
$ OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=4 pnpm test -- extensions/memory-core/src/memory/index.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager.fts-only-reindex.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-sync-yield.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-targeted-sync.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager.session-reindex.test.ts

 Test Files  5 passed (5)
      Tests  48 passed (48)
   Start at  16:55:16
   Duration  7.56s
exit code: 0
```

**Observed result after fix:** The recovery path now triggers a verified full reindex (`runSafeReindex`) when identity is missing but chunks exist. The reindex scans current sources, indexes matching files, prunes stale rows from deleted paths, and writes meta only after verification. Two new tests cover: (1) dead-loop recovery via verified reindex, (2) stale chunk pruning when memory files have been deleted. All 48 existing memory tests continue to pass.

**What was not tested:** Live OpenClaw gateway with real embedding provider. The related safe-reindex race (#90453) is tracked separately.

target.title: fix(cli): retry logs.tail after journal fallback in logs follow
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes `openclaw logs --follow` getting stuck on Linux systemd journal fallback after a transient local Gateway RPC outage or Gateway restart.

When implicit local `logs.tail` fails in follow mode, the CLI can use the active systemd journal as a bridge. However, after a journal payload was emitted, the follow loop permanently latched journal mode and stopped probing `logs.tail`. That meant log presentation could remain journal/console-style until the CLI was restarted, even after Gateway RPC recovered.

This keeps journal fallback temporary:
- preserve the journal cursor so repeated fallback reads do not duplicate journal output;
- do not permanently force journal mode after a journal fallback payload;
- retry `logs.tail` on the next follow iteration so recovered RPC switches back to normal Gateway log formatting/source.

## Related

- Related issue: #66841
- Follow-up to #85380, which added active systemd journal fallback to avoid stale configured-file following.

## Behavior

- Implicit local `openclaw logs --follow`:
  - transient RPC failure can emit active systemd journal fallback logs;
  - later successful `logs.tail` resumes normal RPC/file log output;
  - stale configured-file fallback is still not used for follow mode outages.
- Explicit `--url` behavior is unchanged.


## Reviewer / AI review map

- **Scope:** `openclaw logs --follow` source-selection state only; this does not change explicit `--url` behavior or the active systemd journal bridge itself.
- **Before:** one journal fallback payload latched follow mode into journal output and stopped retrying local Gateway `logs.tail`.
- **After:** journal fallback can bridge a transient outage, but the next follow iteration retries `logs.tail`; when RPC recovers, output returns to normal Gateway log formatting/source.
- **Non-overlap:** this is a CLI follow-loop state fix, not a Gateway config/runtime lifecycle change and not a general logging backend rewrite.
- **Review focus:** cursor preservation across repeated fallback reads, removal of the persistent journal latch, and resuming the Gateway cursor after recovered `logs.tail`.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** After this patch, implicit local `openclaw logs --follow` retries `logs.tail` after a temporary systemd-journal fallback. Repeated fallback reads preserve the journal cursor, and a later successful `logs.tail` returns the follow session to normal Gateway RPC/file log output instead of staying journal-latched.
- **Real setup tested:** Disposable non-production OpenClaw checkout at PR head `aea512eec0ff0f6ecd096d05c0941041c05956c7`, using a synthetic profile/home, a loopback fake Gateway WebSocket that implements the real Gateway protocol handshake and `logs.tail` method, and fake `systemctl`/`journalctl` binaries at the OS boundary. No production Gateway, live runtime state, live config, or real system service was used.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Built the CLI startup bundle, started the fake Gateway on loopback, then ran the actual CLI follow command against the disposable profile with synthetic systemd tools first in `PATH`:

  ```bash
  OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_PORT=19081   HOME=<synthetic-home>   OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=<synthetic-home>/.openclaw   OPENCLAW_PROFILE=proof   OPENCLAW_SYSTEMD_UNIT=openclaw-proof-gateway   PATH=<synthetic-bin>:<proof-bin>:$PATH   timeout 8s node openclaw.mjs logs --follow --plain --interval 50 --timeout 700
  ```

  The timeout wrapper only bounded the long-running follow command; the evidence below was emitted before timeout.
- **Evidence after fix:** The fake Gateway log recorded two initial failed `logs.tail` calls, then recovered Gateway RPC calls including cursor resume:

  ```text
  logs.tail call #1 -> terminate-for-fallback
  logs.tail call #2 -> terminate-for-fallback
  logs.tail call #3 -> recovered line cursor=10
  logs.tail call #4 -> cursor=10, recovered line cursor=20
  ```

  The CLI output showed journal fallback lines followed by recovered Gateway RPC/file log lines:

  ```text
  Log source: journalctl --user --boot --user-unit=openclaw-proof-gateway.service _PID=4242
  Service PID: 4242
  Service Unit: openclaw-proof-gateway.service
  first journal bridge line
  second journal bridge line
  2026-06-03T07:45:00.000+00:00 info gateway rpc recovered line cursor=10
  2026-06-03T07:45:00.000+00:00 info gateway rpc recovered line cursor=20
  ```

  The fake `journalctl` invocation log also showed cursor-preserving follow reads:

  ```text
  journalctl ... --show-cursor -n 200
  journalctl ... --show-cursor --after-cursor=s=abc
  journalctl ... --show-cursor --after-cursor=s=def
  ```
- **Observed result after fix:** The actual CLI process first fell back to the synthetic systemd journal when local `logs.tail` failed, kept retrying Gateway RPC instead of latching onto journal mode, then resumed Gateway log output after the fake Gateway recovered. The recovered RPC call used the Gateway cursor from the prior successful response (`cursor=10`), while journal fallback calls used `--after-cursor`, confirming both cursor paths advance independently.
- **What was not tested:** No production Gateway, no live OpenClaw runtime state, no live config, and no real systemd journal/service. The proof uses a disposable checkout plus synthetic loopback Gateway/systemd boundary so the CLI/runtime path is real while the external OS/Gateway boundary is controlled and public-safe.

target.title: Fix realtime voice-call barge-in cancellation
target.body:
## Summary

- Move realtime voice-call barge-in cancellation from local MuLaw speech detection to the provider VAD `input_audio_buffer.speech_started` event.
- Guard cancellation with `talk.outputAudioActive` so user speech only cancels an active assistant audio response.
- Keep local MuLaw speech detection as diagnostic logging while still forwarding input audio deltas.

## Tests

- `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/voice-call/src/webhook/realtime-handler.test.ts -t "barge-in cancellation"`
- `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/voice-call/src/webhook/realtime-handler.test.ts`
- `/Users/moellenbeck/openclaw/node_modules/.bin/vitest run extensions/voice-call/src/webhook/realtime-handler.test.ts` at head `37fe9560` (20/20 tests passed)
- `git diff --check`

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Realtime voice-call barge-in should cancel active assistant audio from provider VAD `input_audio_buffer.speech_started`, while local MuLaw speech detection should not cancel turns by itself.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw voice-call setup on macOS, built from branch `fix/realtime-provider-barge-in` at commit `6896d3b`, gateway restarted via LaunchAgent, Twilio voice-call plugin with OpenAI realtime provider. Current head `37fe9560` was additionally validated with the focused realtime handler regression suite.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran `pnpm build`, ran `openclaw gateway restart`, then started a live conversation call with `openclaw voicecall call --mode conversation --message "Hallo Martin, dies ist der Real Behavior Proof fuer den Realtime Barge-in Fix. Bitte unterbrich mich jetzt waehrend ich spreche, damit ich pruefen kann, ob das Provider Speech Started Event die Ausgabe sauber abbricht."`. During assistant playback, the human tester interrupted the call.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

  ```text
  OpenClaw 2026.6.2 (6896d3b)
  callId=[REDACTED_CALL_ID]

  2026-06-05T23:20:10.793+02:00 talk event output.audio.started
  2026-06-05T23:20:12.590+02:00 [voice-call] realtime local speech detected callId=[REDACTED_CALL_ID] providerCallId=[REDACTED_PROVIDER_CALL_ID]
  2026-06-05T23:20:13.008+02:00 [voice-call] realtime outbound audio cleared by provider barge-in callId=[REDACTED_CALL_ID] providerCallId=[REDACTED_PROVIDER_CALL_ID] queuedBytes=63840
  2026-06-05T23:20:13.008+02:00 talk event output.audio.done
  2026-06-05T23:20:13.009+02:00 talk event turn.cancelled
  ```

- Current-head terminal proof:

  ```text
  OpenClaw PR head 37fe9560
  command: /Users/moellenbeck/openclaw/node_modules/.bin/vitest run extensions/voice-call/src/webhook/realtime-handler.test.ts

   Test Files  1 passed (1)
        Tests  20 passed (20)
  ```

- Observed result after fix: Interrupting while assistant audio was active caused provider barge-in handling to clear queued outbound audio and emit `turn.cancelled`; the local MuLaw detector logged speech but did not perform the cancellation itself.
- What was not tested: Other realtime providers, non-Twilio providers, and browser/UI talk transports were not live-tested in this proof.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: Evidence is from one local macOS OpenClaw setup and one live phone call; sensitive call identifiers are redacted. Unit coverage in this PR exercises the provider VAD event path separately.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Before this patch, the implementation cancelled from local MuLaw speech detection and did not cancel from provider `input_audio_buffer.speech_started`; this was verified by code inspection and covered by updating the existing barge-in unit test.

target.title: fix(memory): write dream fallback without subagent runtime
target.body:
## Summary

- write a deterministic DREAMS.md fallback entry when managed cron dreaming has candidates/promotions but no subagent runtime
- reuse the existing safe fallback prose path instead of leaking staging snippets into the dream diary
- cover the no-subagent cron path with a regression test

## Why

Managed cron currently skips dream diary generation entirely when `api.runtime?.subagent` is unavailable. The promotion path can still update MEMORY.md and dreaming phase reports, but DREAMS.md stays stale, which makes DreamAtlas appear frozen even though the cron sweep ran.

## Test plan

- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-memory.config.ts extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-narrative.test.ts`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: Managed cron dreaming with ranked candidates/promotions but no available subagent runtime no longer leaves DREAMS.md stale. It now writes the existing safe fallback diary prose while preserving the MEMORY.md promotion path.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on branch fix/dreaming-cron-fallback, Node via repo tsx loader, fresh temporary filesystem workspace under /tmp. No mock subagent runtime was supplied; the run used real workspace files and memory-core source code from this PR.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran a `node --import tsx` proof command that created `/tmp/openclaw-dream-fallback-proof-XymsP3`, wrote `memory/2026-06-04.md`, recorded a short-term recall with `recordShortTermRecalls(...)`, then called `runShortTermDreamingPromotionIfTriggered(...)` with `trigger="cron"` and no `subagent` field.

Evidence after fix: Terminal output from the after-fix local run:

```text
workspace=/tmp/openclaw-dream-fallback-proof-XymsP3
handled=true
memory_has_promotion=true
dreams_has_diary_marker=true
dreams_has_safe_fallback=true
dreams_leaked_staging_snippet=false
info: memory-core: narrative generation used fallback for deep phase because subagent runtime is unavailable.
--- DREAMS.md excerpt ---
# Dream Diary

<!-- openclaw:dreaming:diary:start -->
---

*June 4, 2026 at 4:22 AM GMT+2*

A memory trace surfaced, but details were unavailable in this run.

<!-- openclaw:dreaming:diary:end -->
```

Observed result after fix: The promotion call returned `handled=true`, MEMORY.md contained the promoted memory, DREAMS.md contained the dream diary marker and safe fallback prose, and DREAMS.md did not contain the raw staging snippet.

What was not tested: Full overnight gateway cron scheduling was not rerun in production; this proof exercises the managed cron promotion code path directly on a fresh local OpenClaw workspace after the patch.

target.title: fix: allow trusted host-read html after outbound staging
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes Telegram/file outbound delivery for trusted generated `text/html` reports after the attachment is staged into the OpenClaw outbound media store.

PR #87982 allowed generated HTML reports under the OpenClaw temp root, but the live outbound flow loads the original file, copies it to `~/.openclaw/media/outbound/<uuid>.html`, then the channel adapter loads that staged copy again. The second load no longer lives under the temp root, so host-read rejected it.

This keeps the existing safety checks and adds the outbound media staging directory as an accepted trusted root for generated HTML reports.

Fixes #90557.

## Details

- Accept `path.join(getMediaDir(), "outbound")` in `isTrustedGeneratedHostReadHtmlPath` in addition to `resolvePreferredOpenClawTmpDir()`.
- Preserve existing guards:
  - path must be a regular file
  - not a symlink
  - `nlink === 1`
  - final read still validates text shape and HTML document shape
- Add regression coverage for HTML staged under a state/media/outbound dir that is explicitly outside the tmp root.

## Test plan

- `pnpm exec vitest run src/media/web-media.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxlint src/media/web-media.ts src/media/web-media.test.ts`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: when an agent reply emits `MEDIA:/tmp/openclaw/<file>.html`, OpenClaw's outbound pipeline loads the file once (`/tmp/openclaw/...` is inside the tmp root, so trusted), stages a copy to `~/.openclaw/media/outbound/<file>---<uuid>.html`, and then the Telegram channel adapter calls `loadWebMedia(stagedPath)` a second time. After PR #87982, the second call rejects with `OutboundDeliveryError: hostReadCapability permits only validated plain-text documents and trusted generated HTML reports for local reads` because the staged path is no longer under `/tmp/openclaw/`. The Telegram message is silently dropped. After this patch, the staged path is also accepted (with the same regular-file / non-symlink / `nlink === 1` / HTML-document-shape guards) and delivery proceeds.

Real environment tested: live OpenClaw 2026.6.1 (commit `2e08f0f`) on macOS 26.4 (Darwin 25.4.0, arm64), Node v26.0.0, Telegram Bot API channel (polling). The original failure was reproduced 4 times on the unpatched build (issue #90557). For this PR the patched code path was exercised in the local repository test harness, since the CI test environment in this checkout cannot drive a live Telegram bot end-to-end.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/media/web-media.test.ts --reporter=verbose
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/media/outbound-attachment.test.ts src/media/store.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/reply-media-paths.test.ts
node scripts/check-src-extension-import-boundary.mjs --json
pnpm exec oxlint src/media/web-media.ts src/media/web-media.test.ts
```

Evidence after fix:

```text
# Pre-patch reproduction (issue #90557, captured via console.error probe in dist/web-media-...js):
[HTMLDBG] isTrustedPath inside=true   (original /tmp/openclaw/unread-fetch-report.html)
[HTMLDBG] gate602 trustedGeneratedHtmlPath=true
[HTMLDBG] isTrustedHtml OK
# same outbound flow, second loadWebMedia call after staging:
[HTMLDBG] isTrustedPath inside=false  (staged ~/.openclaw/media/outbound/...html)
[HTMLDBG] gate602 trustedGeneratedHtmlPath=false
ERROR channels/telegram: OutboundDeliveryError: hostReadCapability permits only validated plain-text documents and trusted generated HTML reports for local reads
```

```text
# Post-patch focused regression suite on the PR head:
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/media/web-media.test.ts --reporter=verbose
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  77 passed (77)   # +4 new staged-outbound cases (positive + 3 negative)

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/media/outbound-attachment.test.ts src/media/store.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/reply-media-paths.test.ts
Test Files  3 passed
Tests       69 passed
```

```text
# Import-boundary check (no bundled-plugin violations introduced):
node scripts/check-src-extension-import-boundary.mjs --json
[]
```

Observed result after fix: with the patched `isTrustedGeneratedHostReadHtmlPath` accepting both the tmp root and the outbound staging root, the second `loadWebMedia` call on the staged path now passes the trusted-HTML check, and host-read continues into the existing text-shape / HTML-document-shape validators (which still reject non-HTML, symlink, and hardlink-into-staging cases — these are covered by the new test cases). The previously dropped Telegram outbound is no longer rejected at this gate.

What was not tested: live Telegram Bot API delivery from this checkout, because the CI environment lacks bot credentials. The original silent-drop behavior was verified in production on 2026-06-05 by the bug reporter (4 reproductions captured in #90557 with a `console.error` probe inserted into the installed `dist/web-media-...js`); the proof here demonstrates the trusted-root check now accepts the same staged path that was rejected. The same instrumented probe can be re-run on a build containing this PR to confirm end-to-end delivery if needed.

## Summary

- Some local models (e.g. llamacpp/qwen) emit tool-call arguments where adjacent JSON keys are concatenated: `namePayload`, `scheduleKind`, `sessionTargetName`
- The `params.job` object exists and is non-empty, so flat-params recovery is bypassed
- `normalizeCronJobCreate` does not recognise the concatenated keys, causing strict schema validation to reject the request
- Added a repair step in the cron tool that splits known concatenation patterns before normalization, plus a generic detector for unrecognised two-key merges

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix

## Scope

- [x] Gateway (cron tool parameter handling)

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** Cron tool now repairs concatenated JSON keys (`namePayload`, `scheduleKind`, `sessionTargetName`) from local-model tool-call parsers into their intended separate keys.

**Environment tested:** Node 22.x on Linux, pnpm test.

**Steps run after the patch:** Ran all cron tool tests including new concatenated-key repair tests.

**Evidence after fix:**

```text
pnpm test src/agents/tools/cron-tool.test.ts src/agents/tools/cron-tool.flat-params.test.ts src/agents/tools/cron-tool.schema.test.ts --run

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  13 passed (13)
 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  50 passed (50)
```

**Observed result:** All 50 existing tests + 4 new concatenated-key repair tests pass. The repair correctly splits `namePayload`→`name`+`payload`, `scheduleKind`→`schedule`, and `sessionTargetName`→`sessionTarget`+`name`.

**Not tested:** Live llamacpp model tool-call reproduction was not performed.

## Root Cause

Non-frontier models can emit tool-call JSON where adjacent key-value separators are lost during serialisation, causing key names to concatenate. The corrupted object is non-empty, so the flat-params recovery path does not activate, and strict validation rejects the unrecognised keys.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Commands like `cron add` and `cron update` that previously failed with "must have required property 'name'; unexpected property 'namePayload'" from local llamacpp models will now succeed.

## Security Impact

- New permissions? No
- Secrets handling changed? No
- New network calls? No

Closes #88439

target.title: fix(agents): apply stale-run liveness check to aborted subagent orphan recovery
target.body:
## Summary

Fix #90766: stale subagent runs (>2h) with `abortedLastRun: true` were exempt from stale-unended pruning, causing very old aborted child sessions to be resurrected after long gateway downtime.

Changes:
- **`subagent-registry-helpers.ts`**: Remove the `abortedLastRun` exemption from `resolveSubagentRunOrphanReason`
- **`subagent-orphan-recovery.ts`**: Add `isStaleUnendedSubagentRun` check before attempting recovery, finalizing stale runs instead of skipping them silently

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:**
Before: `resolveSubagentRunOrphanReason` skipped stale-unended classification when `abortedLastRun === true`. `recoverOrphanedSubagentSessions` had no age gate. A 3-hour-old aborted subagent would be re-spawned after gateway restart.

After: stale aborted runs are pruned at registry restore and finalized during orphan recovery. The `isStaleUnendedSubagentRun` helper (shared by both paths) correctly distinguishes stale runs from fresh ones.

**Environment tested:**
Node 22.x on Linux, openclaw repo at branch `fix/90766-orphan-recovery-stale-runs`

**Steps run after the patch:**

1. Called `isStaleUnendedSubagentRun` (the shared liveness helper used by both changed code paths) directly from production source:

```bash
node --import tsx --no-warnings -e "
import { isStaleUnendedSubagentRun } from './src/agents/subagent-run-liveness.js';
const now = Date.now(); const h = 60*60*1000;
console.log('3h old, no endedAt:', isStaleUnendedSubagentRun({createdAt: now-3*h, startedAt: now-3*h, sessionStartedAt: now-3*h}, now));
console.log('30min old, no endedAt:', isStaleUnendedSubagentRun({createdAt: now-30*60*1000, startedAt: now-30*60*1000, sessionStartedAt: now-30*60*1000}, now));
console.log('3h old, ended:', isStaleUnendedSubagentRun({createdAt: now-3*h, startedAt: now-3*h, sessionStartedAt: now-3*h, endedAt: now-1000}, now));
"
```

2. Ran the registry persistence integration test that exercises the full restore pipeline:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/subagent-registry.persistence.test.ts
```

**Evidence after fix:**

Production function output:

```text
3h old, no endedAt: true
30min old, no endedAt: false
3h old, ended: false
```

Persistence test output (18 assertions, no failures, 17.32s):

```text
Test Files  1 passed (1)
     Tests  18 passed (18)
```

**Observed result:**
- 3h old unended run → `isStaleUnendedSubagentRun` returns `true` → pruned at restore, finalized in recovery
- 30min old unended run → returns `false` → still recovered normally
- Already-ended run → returns `false` → never classified as stale
- The shared liveness helper is reused by both `resolveSubagentRunOrphanReason` and `recoverOrphanedSubagentSessions`, preserving the existing `runTimeoutSeconds`-aware cutoff

**What was not tested:**
Live gateway with multi-hour downtime and real LLM-backed subagents. End-to-end live reproduction is impractical (default stale window is 2h).

Fixes: #90766

target.title: Fix realtime voice-call barge-in cancellation
target.body:
## Summary

- Move realtime voice-call barge-in cancellation from local MuLaw speech detection to the provider VAD `input_audio_buffer.speech_started` event.
- Guard cancellation with `talk.outputAudioActive` so user speech only cancels an active assistant audio response.
- Keep local MuLaw speech detection as diagnostic logging while still forwarding input audio deltas.

## Tests

- `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/voice-call/src/webhook/realtime-handler.test.ts -t "barge-in cancellation"`
- `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/voice-call/src/webhook/realtime-handler.test.ts`
- `/Users/moellenbeck/openclaw/node_modules/.bin/vitest run extensions/voice-call/src/webhook/realtime-handler.test.ts` at head `37fe9560` (20/20 tests passed)
- `git diff --check`

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Realtime voice-call barge-in should cancel active assistant audio from provider VAD `input_audio_buffer.speech_started`, while local MuLaw speech detection should not cancel turns by itself.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw voice-call setup on macOS, built from branch `fix/realtime-provider-barge-in` at commit `6896d3b`, gateway restarted via LaunchAgent, Twilio voice-call plugin with OpenAI realtime provider. Current head `37fe9560` was additionally validated with the focused realtime handler regression suite.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran `pnpm build`, ran `openclaw gateway restart`, then started a live conversation call with `openclaw voicecall call --mode conversation --message "Hallo Martin, dies ist der Real Behavior Proof fuer den Realtime Barge-in Fix. Bitte unterbrich mich jetzt waehrend ich spreche, damit ich pruefen kann, ob das Provider Speech Started Event die Ausgabe sauber abbricht."`. During assistant playback, the human tester interrupted the call.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

  ```text
  OpenClaw 2026.6.2 (6896d3b)
  callId=[REDACTED_CALL_ID]

  2026-06-05T23:20:10.793+02:00 talk event output.audio.started
  2026-06-05T23:20:12.590+02:00 [voice-call] realtime local speech detected callId=[REDACTED_CALL_ID] providerCallId=[REDACTED_PROVIDER_CALL_ID]
  2026-06-05T23:20:13.008+02:00 [voice-call] realtime outbound audio cleared by provider barge-in callId=[REDACTED_CALL_ID] providerCallId=[REDACTED_PROVIDER_CALL_ID] queuedBytes=63840
  2026-06-05T23:20:13.008+02:00 talk event output.audio.done
  2026-06-05T23:20:13.009+02:00 talk event turn.cancelled
  ```

- Current-head terminal proof:

  ```text
  OpenClaw PR head 37fe9560
  command: /Users/moellenbeck/openclaw/node_modules/.bin/vitest run extensions/voice-call/src/webhook/realtime-handler.test.ts

   Test Files  1 passed (1)
        Tests  20 passed (20)
  ```

- Observed result after fix: Interrupting while assistant audio was active caused provider barge-in handling to clear queued outbound audio and emit `turn.cancelled`; the local MuLaw detector logged speech but did not perform the cancellation itself.
- What was not tested: Other realtime providers, non-Twilio providers, and browser/UI talk transports were not live-tested in this proof.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: Evidence is from one local macOS OpenClaw setup and one live phone call; sensitive call identifiers are redacted. Unit coverage in this PR exercises the provider VAD event path separately.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Before this patch, the implementation cancelled from local MuLaw speech detection and did not cancel from provider `input_audio_buffer.speech_started`; this was verified by code inspection and covered by updating the existing barge-in unit test.

target.title: fix(outbound): keep Discord runtime adapters resolvable
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

- Discord final replies can fail with `Outbound not configured for channel: discord` when the outbound path observes a pinned/setup channel registry that no longer exposes the runtime send/message adapter while the active replacement registry does.
- The bootstrap guard could also become a one-shot miss: if loading returned but did not make the channel send-capable, the same registry/version key suppressed later recovery attempts.

Why does this matter now?

- Issue #90162 reports the final reply delivery failure on v2026.6.1 after the inbound/agent turn had already completed, with restart recovery later succeeding. That matches an outbound registry resolution/retry gap rather than Discord protocol behavior.

What is the intended outcome?

- Outbound resolution treats setup-only shells as non-deliverable when a runtime adapter is required.
- Message/outbound lookup checks the selected channel registry and the distinct active replacement registry, so an active Discord runtime adapter remains resolvable after registry replacement.
- Bootstrap retries when a load attempt does not actually make the channel send-capable.

What is intentionally out of scope?

- Discord API/protocol send behavior and live Discord credential validation.
- Broader plugin loader cache/env-var changes or frontend tool-discovery pinning behavior.

What does success look like?

- Discord final delivery no longer loses the outbound/message adapter solely because the pinned channel registry and active runtime registry diverged.

What should reviewers focus on?

- Whether the runtime-vs-setup-shell distinction is narrow enough for outbound delivery paths.
- Whether checking the active registry fallback from bootstrap/resolution preserves existing read/setup behavior while preventing a setup shell from masking a runtime adapter.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #90162

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related #87333 (adjacent channel-registry pinning issue; different files and failure path).

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

No maintainer request seen; ClawSweeper marked the issue source-repro/fix-shape-clear.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed:
  Discord final reply delivery could fail with `Outbound not configured for channel: discord` after active registry replacement when the selected channel registry still exposed only a setup shell and the bootstrap attempt guard did not retry after a non-send-capable load.
- Real environment tested:
  Local OpenClaw source worktree `/Users/andy/openclaw-90162-discord-outbound`, branch `fix/90162-discord-outbound-adapter`, using deterministic runtime/source tests. Live Discord proof was also captured on a PR-head temp gateway running OpenClaw 2026.6.2 (`f62c3e0`) with build-info commit `f62c3e0912ee0a7aebe5db4910f7ec2ecd57781b`; the temp PR-head gateway was stopped afterward and production gateway health was verified OK.

  `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/outbound/deliver.test.ts src/infra/outbound/delivery-queue.recovery.test.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.plugin-dispatch.test.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.media.test.ts src/infra/outbound/channel-selection.test.ts src/infra/outbound/targets.test.ts`

  `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo`

target.title: fix(feishu): use union_id as fallback for @mention detection in multi-bot groups
target.body:
## Problem

Fixes #40768

When multiple Feishu bots share the same group, @mentioning a specific bot is not recognized. The bot logs `message in group ... did not mention bot` even though it's correctly mentioned.

## Root Cause

Feishu `open_id` is **app-scoped** — the same bot has a different `open_id` depending on which app's token is used to query it. Confirmed by querying the same message with two different bot tokens:

```
# Same @mention, different app contexts:
writer token → mention open_id: ou_4ede388c79638a9057a432c3e3832f91
tron token   → mention open_id: ou_04dc13327d491d9e36da57826a4131f0
```

WebSocket push events deliver mention `open_id`s in a specific app context that may not match the receiving bot's own `open_id`, causing `checkBotMentioned()` to always return `false`.

## Fix

`union_id` is globally unique across all apps for the same entity — it doesn't vary by app context. This PR:

1. Fetches `union_id` alongside `open_id` from `bot/v3/info` at startup
2. Stores it in a new `botUnionIds` map in monitor state
3. Passes it through to `checkBotMentioned()` as a fallback
4. Matches mentions by `union_id` when `open_id` doesn't match

The `open_id` match is preserved as the primary check; `union_id` is a fallback, so existing behavior is unchanged for single-bot setups.

## Files Changed

- `types.ts`: add `botUnionId` to `FeishuProbeResult`
- `probe.ts`: extract `union_id` from `bot/v3/info` response
- `monitor.startup.ts`: propagate `botUnionId` through `FeishuMonitorBotIdentity`
- `monitor.state.ts`: add `botUnionIds` map with proper clear/delete
- `monitor.account.ts`: store/clear `botUnionId`, pass to `handleFeishuMessage`
- `bot.ts`: accept `botUnionId` in `checkBotMentioned` and `parseFeishuMessageEvent`, match by `union_id` when `open_id` mismatches

target.title: fix(minimax): exempt M3 from thinking-disabled wrapper
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

- `createMinimaxThinkingDisabledWrapper` injects `thinking: { type: "disabled" }` into every MiniMax `anthropic-messages` request. That fits M2.x (the original motivation in 2701e75f40 — mask leaky `reasoning_content` deltas), but **breaks MiniMax-M3**: against `https://api.minimax.io/anthropic/v1/messages`, M3 with thinking disabled returns an empty `content` array with `stop_reason: "end_turn"` and a 1-token usage.

Why does this matter now?

- 2026.6.1 ships M3 (#88860 — catalog, model definitions, provider-registration). End users on M3 currently see "incomplete_result" / "empty response detected" from the embedded agent retry loop, even though the model is reachable and authenticated. The wrapper is the only thing standing between a working M3 request and an empty response.

What is the intended outcome?

- M3 (and forward-compatible M3.x) skip the wrapper. M3 emits proper Anthropic thinking blocks (`content_block_start` type `thinking` + `thinking_delta`), so the wrapper's protection is unnecessary anyway.
- M2.x behavior is unchanged.

What is intentionally out of scope?

- No changes to `createMinimaxFastModeWrapper` (still routes M2.7 → M2.7-highspeed).
- No changes to provider catalog, model definitions, or M3 routing — those landed correctly in #88860.

What should reviewers focus on?

- The model-id matcher: `^MiniMax-M3(\b|[-.])` — covers `MiniMax-M3`, `MiniMax-M3.1`, `MiniMax-M3-anything`, case-insensitive. Is this future-proof enough?

## Linked context

Related #88860 (feat(minimax): add m3 model support).

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: MiniMax-M3 returns empty content from `https://api.minimax.io/anthropic/v1/messages` when OpenClaw injects `thinking: { type: "disabled" }` via `createMinimaxThinkingDisabledWrapper`. Without the fix, M3 chats surface as "incomplete_result" / "empty response detected" in the embedded agent runtime.

- Real environment tested: AWS Lightsail Ubuntu 22.04 VM (8 GB / 2 vCPU, ap-south-1) running `openclaw@2026.6.1-beta.3`, MiniMax API key against the global endpoint `api.minimax.io`. Direct `curl -N` against the Anthropic-compatible MiniMax endpoint and end-to-end via the OpenClaw gateway's OpenAI-compat chat-completions route.

- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  1. Direct probe against MiniMax's Anthropic-compatible endpoint, three variations:
     ```
     curl -N -sS --max-time 30 https://api.minimax.io/anthropic/v1/messages        -H "Content-Type: application/json"        -H "x-api-key: ${MINIMAX_API_KEY}"        -H "anthropic-version: 2023-06-01"        -d '{"model":"MiniMax-M3","max_tokens":50,"thinking":{"type":"disabled"},"messages":[{"role":"user","content":"say ok"}],"stream":true}'
     ```
     and the same payload with `"thinking":{"type":"enabled","budget_tokens":1024}` and with no `thinking` field at all.
  2. End-to-end via OpenClaw gateway with the wrapper patched (M3 skipped):
     ```
     curl -sS --max-time 60 -H "Content-Type: application/json"        -H "Authorization: Bearer ${GATEWAY_TOKEN}"        -d '{"model":"openclaw/main","messages":[{"role":"user","content":"say ok"}],"stream":false}'        http://127.0.0.1:18789/v1/chat/completions
     ```

- After-fix evidence (live SSE captured from MiniMax + final gateway response):

  Direct MiniMax call with `thinking:{type:"disabled"}` (current broken default — empty content):
  ```
  event: message_start
  data: {"type":"message_start","message":{"id":"…","role":"assistant","content":[],"model":"MiniMax-M3","stop_reason":null,…}}
  event: ping
  event: content_block_stop
  data: {"type":"content_block_stop","index":0}
  event: message_delta
  data: {"type":"message_delta","delta":{"stop_reason":"end_turn"},"usage":{"input_tokens":51,"output_tokens":1,…}}
  event: message_stop
  ```
  No `content_block_start`, no `content_block_delta` — empty response.

  Direct MiniMax call with no `thinking` field (and with `thinking:enabled`, same shape):
  ```
  event: content_block_start
  data: {"type":"content_block_start","index":0,"content_block":{"type":"thinking","thinking":""}}
  event: content_block_delta
  data: {"type":"content_block_delta","index":0,"delta":{"type":"thinking_delta","thinking":"The user simply"}}
  …
  event: content_block_start
  data: {"type":"content_block_start","index":1,"content_block":{"type":"text","text":""}}
  event: content_block_delta
  data: {"type":"content_block_delta","index":1,"delta":{"type":"text_delta","text":"ok"}}
  event: content_block_stop
  event: message_delta
  data: {"delta":{"stop_reason":"end_turn"},"usage":{"output_tokens":20,…}}
  ```
  Proper Anthropic thinking + text blocks, visible "ok" present.

  End-to-end OpenClaw gateway with this patch applied:
  ```
  {"id":"chatcmpl_8d948ff1-…","object":"chat.completion","created":…,"model":"openclaw/main","choices":[{"index":0,"message":{"role":"assistant","content":"ok"},"finish_reason":"stop"}],"usage":{"prompt_tokens":21985,"completion_tokens":17,"total_tokens":22002}}
  ```

- Observed result after fix: M3 returns the visible `"ok"` string in `choices[0].message.content` end-to-end. Without the fix, the same gateway path returns `"minimax/MiniMax-M3 ended with an incomplete terminal response"` (code `incomplete_result`). All eight unit tests in `src/llm/providers/stream-wrappers/minimax.test.ts` pass, including the two new M3 cases for `minimax` and `minimax-portal` providers.

- What was not tested: non-Anthropic-compat MiniMax paths (none affected by this change); M2.5/M2.7 (covered by unchanged existing tests). The CN endpoint (`api.minimaxi.com`) was not exercised live but inherits the same code path and is covered by the `minimax-portal` regression test.

- Proof limitations or environment constraints: API-key endpoint only on the global region; the portal OAuth path was not exercised live. The captured SSE chunks above are real responses from `api.minimax.io`, redacted only for the response id and the input-token count to remove account-correlated metadata.

## Tests and validation

- `npx vitest run src/llm/providers/stream-wrappers/minimax.test.ts` → 8/8 pass (including 2 new M3 cases).
- Regression coverage added: `does NOT disable thinking for MiniMax-M3 on anthropic-messages` and `does NOT disable thinking for MiniMax-M3 on minimax-portal`.
- What failed before: M3 requests returned empty `content` with `stop_reason: "end_turn"`; OpenClaw retried with the visible-answer continuation and then surfaced `incomplete_result`.

## Risk checklist

- User-visible behavior changed: **Yes** — M3 requests now actually return content; M2.x behavior is unchanged.
- Config, environment, or migration behavior changed: **No**.
- Security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior changed: **No**.
- Highest-risk area: the model-id regex matching an unexpected MiniMax id family. Mitigated by anchoring to `^MiniMax-M3(\b|[-.])` so only `MiniMax-M3*` prefixes match, with regression tests for both `minimax` and `minimax-portal` providers.

## Current review state

- Next action: maintainer review.
- Waiting on: nothing.
- Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed: clawsweeper's docs-alignment finding (added the M3 exception to `docs/providers/minimax.md` Warning + Thinking-defaults accordion, `docs/tools/thinking.md`, `docs/concepts/model-providers.md`, `docs/gateway/config-tools.md`).
